export_requirements.export_permit: Required for all live animal exports. Apply through provincial State Veterinarian office.

import_requirements.microchip: Required (Universal scanner detectable). MUST be implanted BEFORE rabies vaccination or vaccine is considered invalid
import_requirements.quarantine: Quarantine may be shortened if dog receives US-licensed rabies vaccine and meets titre requirements during quarantine
import_requirements.import_permit: No formal import permit. CDC Dog Import Form is the registration mechanism.
import_requirements.health_certificate: No standalone health certificate requirement. The CDC certification forms include health attestation.

airlines.name: Emirates
airlines.policy_summary: Cargo: Emirates SkyCargo handles all pet transport as manifested cargo. Snub-nosed (brachycephalic) breeds accepted with additional requirements and seasonal restrictions. Temperature embargoes during extreme heat (summer months in Gulf region)

airlines.name: Delta Air Lines
airlines.policy_summary: Small pets allowed in cabin. Cargo transport available

airlines.name: Virgin Atlantic
airlines.policy_summary: Cargo: Pets transported through Virgin Atlantic Cargo. Brachycephalic breeds restricted. Temperature embargoes

timeline_steps.timing: Before any vaccinations. Must be done first.
At least 21 days before travel (after microchip)
Within 10 days of travel (check destination-specific requirements)

timeline_steps.action: Book IATA-compliant flight and arrange pet cargo/cabin booking
timeline_steps.timing: 2-4 weeks before travel. Confirm pet space availability.

timeline_steps.action: Travel day: arrive early, present all documentation
timeline_steps.timing: 3-4 hours before departure for cargo pets

cost_factors: Airline cargo/cabin fees (varies by carrier, pet weight, and crate size)
Pet transport agent fee (if using a relocation service)
key_warnings: Brachycephalic (flat-faced) breeds face cargo restrictions on most airlines. Check breed-specific policies before booking.
Microchip MUST be implanted before rabies vaccination. Vaccination given before microchip is invalid and the clock restarts.

South Africa is classified as a CDC high-risk country for dog rabies. Additional requirements apply: dogs must arrive at a CDC-registered animal care facility, have FAVN titre test results, and a CDC Dog Import Form.

seo.title: Pet Transport from South Africa to United States | Complete Guide
seo.description: How to transport your dog or cat from South Africa to United States. Import requirements, airline options, costs, and step-by-step process.

content.h1: Pet Transport from South Africa to United States
content.overview:
Transporting your cat from South Africa to United States involves several steps, but thousands of families do it every year.

Expect to start preparations 6-10 weeks before your travel date. Airlines serving this route include Emirates, British Airways, Delta Air Lines, and Virgin Atlantic.

We've put together this guide to walk you through every step.

sections.heading: Key things to know about this route
sections.body: Compared to some international routes, South Africa to United States is relatively straightforward. That doesn't mean you can leave it to the last minute.

sections.heading: Practical tips for shipping your pet from South Africa to United States
sections.body:
Start the microchip and vaccination process as early as possible. The biggest mistake people make is leaving it too late. Once you miss a deadline, the entire timeline pushes back.

Some airlines on this route (Delta Air Lines) allow small pets in the cabin. Your pet must fit in a carrier under the seat. Weight limits are strict: usually 8-10kg including the carrier.

Use a pet transport agent if you're not confident handling the paperwork yourself. A good agent knows the current rules, has relationships with airline cargo departments, and can troubleshoot problems before they become disasters.

sections.heading: Travel day: South Africa to United States
sections.body:
Arrive at the airport at least 3 hours before departure if your pet is travelling as cargo. You'll need to present all original documents: health certificate, vaccination records, microchip number, and any import permits.

Your pet's crate must be clean, labelled with your contact details and the consignee's details at the destination, and have food and water containers attached. Live animal stickers and orientation arrows are required.

On arrival in United States, you (or your agent) collect your pet from the cargo terminal. There may be an inspection by veterinary authorities at the airport. Have copies of all paperwork ready.

faqs.question: Does United States require quarantine for pets from South Africa?
faqs.answer: It depends on your specific circumstances. Quarantine may be shortened if dog receives US-licensed rabies vaccine and meets titre requirements during quarantine. Check with the destination authorities or a pet transport specialist for your exact situation.

faqs.question: Which airlines transport pets from South Africa to United States?
faqs.answer: Airlines operating pet cargo on this route include Emirates, British Airways, Delta Air Lines, Virgin Atlantic. All transport pets as manifested cargo in temperature-controlled holds, not in the cabin. You'll need an IATA-compliant crate and should book cargo space well in advance. Some airlines have seasonal restrictions and brachycephalic breed embargoes.

faqs.question: How far in advance should I start preparing to move my pet from South Africa to United States?
faqs.answer: Start at least 6-10 weeks before your planned travel date. The process begins with microchipping, then rabies vaccination (with a mandatory waiting period), then any required blood tests, health certificates, and permit applications. Rushing the timeline is the most common reason people hit problems.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to transport a dog from South Africa to United States?
faqs.answer: The total cost depends on your dog's size, the airline, and whether you use a transport agent. For a medium-sized dog, expect to pay for: veterinary fees (vaccinations, health certificate, microchip), an IATA-compliant crate, airline cargo fees, government endorsements, and any import permits or quarantine fees at the destination. Using a door-to-door agent adds to the cost but handles the complexity for you.

faqs.question: What documents does my pet need to travel from South Africa to United States?
faqs.answer: Your pet will need: proof of ISO microchip, rabies vaccination certificate, official health certificate. The health certificate must be issued by an official veterinarian within 10 days of travel. Some documents need government endorsement. Check every requirement against the current rules, because regulations change and an outdated document can mean your pet doesn't fly.

faqs.question: Can I fly my French Bulldog or Pug from South Africa to United States?
faqs.answer: Brachycephalic (flat-faced) breeds face restrictions on most airlines, particularly for cargo transport. These breeds are more vulnerable to breathing difficulties at altitude and in heat. Many airlines impose seasonal embargoes (typically summer months), and some restrict flat-faced breeds from cargo year-round. A few airlines still accept them with additional veterinary clearance. Check the specific airline's current policy before booking.

faqs.question: Is it easier to transport a cat from South Africa to United States than a dog?
faqs.answer: Generally, yes. Cats face fewer regulatory hurdles than dogs in most countries. The US has no federal import requirements for cats at all, which makes the process significantly simpler.
